title: Connelly Early once again gave losing Red Sox a chance to win, but failed to pitch deeper into the game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Connelly Early allowed one run in 4.1 innings during Red Sox's 3-2 loss to the Cardinals.
Early struggled with command, compiling a high pitch count of 86 without completing the fifth inning.
Zack Kelly relieved Early, but allowed the Cardinals to secure a 3-2 victory over the Red Sox.

title: It’s been decades since a center won the NBA Most Improved Player award. Will Neemias Queta be next?
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Neemias Queta of the Boston Celtics is a leading candidate for the NBA Most Improved Player award.
Queta averaged 25.5 minutes per game, played in 75 games, and recorded 17 double-doubles this season.
The Celtics secured the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference, with Queta's contributions being a key factor.

title: The Red Sox need to shake up the top of their lineup to avoid slow starts
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox top four batters managed only one hit against Dodgers' Dustin May, leading to a loss.
Consideration is being given to shifting Wilyer Abreu and Roman Anthony to key batting positions.
Possible new leadoff options include Masataka Yoshida and Ceddanne Rafaela to improve early scoring.

title: Celtics clinch No. 2 seed in Eastern Conference playoffs with blowout win over Pelicans
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Celtics clinch No. 2 seed in Eastern Conference with a 144-118 win over Pelicans.
Boston ties NBA's single-game three-point record with 29 threes in victory.
Celtics will face Orlando Magic or Philadelphia 76ers in the first playoff round.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Pizza Movie' starring Gaten Matarazzo and Sean Giambrone is now streaming on Hulu.
'The Boys' Season 5, featuring Homelander's new fascist storyline, is available on Prime Video.
'The Testaments' set in a dystopian Boston is streaming on Hulu, continuing from 'The Handmaid's Tale'.

title: Jayson Tatum won’t play in Celtics’ penultimate regular season matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum will not play in the Celtics' game against the Pelicans due to right Achilles management.
Jaylen Brown will play despite managing left Achilles tendinitis, as confirmed by coach Joe Mazzulla.
Boston could secure the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference with a win against New Orleans or a Knicks loss.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her daughter’s courage through cancer treatment
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lori Robertson, 49, from Concord, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her daughter Emma's cancer treatment journey.
Emma, 16, was diagnosed with medulloblastoma, a brain tumor, leading to significant challenges and extensive treatments.
Emma's treatment team includes MGH professionals and therapy dogs; Lori dedicates her marathon run to them and Emma's bravery.

title: Bruins fans will need to wait a little longer for James Hagens’s NHL debut
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens won't debut for the Bruins against Tampa Bay as they aim to secure a playoff spot.
Coach Marco Sturm emphasizes patience with Hagens, wanting him to acclimate to their system before playing.
Bruins have three regular-season games left, with potential for Hagens to play if coach feels comfortable.

title: Boston billionaires, historic figures land on Forbes’ ‘self-made’ lists
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston billionaires Noubar Afeyan, Bill Cummings, and Herb Chambers feature on Forbes' 'The Greatest Self-Made Americans' list.
Forbes' historic list includes figures with Boston ties such as Benjamin Franklin and Red Sox legends Babe Ruth and Ted Williams.
Forbes' rankings consider financial success, obstacles overcome, and long-term impact, drawing from archives and expert panel insights.

title: What you need to know about the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The 130th Boston Marathon will take place on Patriots Day, April 20, starting in Hopkinton.
This year's race includes six start waves and features 30,000 participants from over 130 countries.
Live coverage of the marathon begins at 4 a.m. on WCVB Channel 5 and through the Very Local app.
